name: Pineapple (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw, ripe Ananas comosus)

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Dominant sugar in ripe pineapple; USDA SR value 5.99 g. Genotype study range 45.2-89.5 mg/g FW (Ma 2018, PMC6270947) brackets this.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Second most abundant sugar; genotype range 13.1-31.4 mg/g FW (PMC6270947).

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Genotype range 13.7-34.9 mg/g FW (PMC6270947). Glucose:fructose:sucrose approx 1:1:3-4.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Ripe pineapple starch is essentially nil (hydrolyzed to free sugars during ripening); reported for completeness.

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence: PREDOMINANT organic acid in pineapple (~60-87% of total acids). Genotype range 1.14-5.78 mg/g FW; midpoint 3.46 mg/g = 0.346 g/100g. Was MISSING from prior profile - major carbon/energy source.

components.name: L-malic acid
components.evidence: Second organic acid; genotype range 0.30-1.61 mg/g FW, midpoint 0.95 mg/g. Present in prior profile as L-Malate.

components.name: quinic acid
components.evidence: Third major organic acid; genotype range 0.50-1.29 mg/g FW, midpoint 0.90 mg/g. Was MISSING from prior profile.

components.name: L-ascorbic acid (vitamin C)
components.evidence: 47.8 mg/100g; genotype study 5.1-33.6 mg/100g. Both organic acid and vitamin C.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Low protein; includes bromelain cysteine proteases characteristic of pineapple.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid in the protein-derived profile (also high free Asn/Asp in pineapple).

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant mineral/cation. Genotype study reports higher whole-fruit values (975-2602 mg/100g dry-ish basis); USDA fresh edible = 109 mg.

components.name: phosphate (as P)
components.evidence: 8 mg elemental P/100g.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Single iron entry; prior profile had 3 redundant BiGG-compartment iron IDs (Fe2+/Fe3+).

components.name: manganese
components.evidence: Notably high in pineapple relative to other fruits.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Ultratrace.

components.name: vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: See organic-acid entry; listed under both roles.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin A carotenoid (Vitamin A RAE = 3 ug). Retinol itself = 0 in this plant food.

components.name: total lipid
components.evidence: Very low fat food.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Principal PUFA; part of the 0.040 g total PUFA.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Remainder of PUFA fraction.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Principal MUFA.

components.name: water
components.evidence: Bulk matrix.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed vitamin A is animal-derived; pineapple (plant food) contains 0 retinol - vitamin A activity is entirely from beta-carotene. FooDB/BiGG artifact.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ion is not a measured growth nutrient of pineapple at meaningful levels; FooDB ultratrace/contaminant noise.

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Non-nutritive ultratrace element, not a documented pineapple nutrient; FooDB noise.

remove.name: Starch C12H20O10
remove.reason: Ripe pineapple starch is essentially 0 (USDA); retained as a 0-value entry only, not a real carbon pool in edible fruit.

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: Redundant compartment-tagged duplicate of iron (BiGG ID artifact); iron kept once.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: Redundant duplicate iron entry (BiGG oxidation-state artifact); iron kept once.

missing_added: citric acid (0.346 g/100g) - dominant organic acid, was entirely absent
quinic acid (0.090 g/100g) - third major organic acid, was absent
specific sugars with real amounts: sucrose 5.99, fructose 2.12, glucose 1.73 g/100g
full quantitative amino acid, vitamin and mineral panel with amounts
keep_uncovered: Serotonin (pineapple is a genuine serotonin-containing fruit)
aroma esters: ethyl acetate, ethyl hexanoate, methyl caprylate, Furaneol, methyl 3-methylbutanoate (real volatiles, negligible for growth)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169124 (Pineapple, raw, all varieties)

Ma, Yang, Wu et al. 2018, 'Physico-Chemical Properties, Antioxidant Activity and Mineral Contents of Pineapple Genotypes Grown in China', Molecules 23(11):2914, PMC6270947 (organic acids, sugars, minerals by HPLC)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe on pineapple are sucrose (~175 mM) plus free fructose and glucose, then the organic-acid pool dominated by citric acid (~18 mM) with malic and quinic acids. Citric and quinic acids were the key growth-relevant omissions in the prior FooDB profile. Manganese is unusually high for a fruit.
